name: Alaska CBPL Mechanical Administrator License Search

agency.name: Alaska Division of Corporations, Business and Professional Licensing

updateFrequency: unknown; verify against the Alaska professional license search before relying on freshness assumptions
licenseTypesIncluded: mechanical administrator professional license records exposed by Alaska CBPL search paths
knownExclusions: The Mechanical Administrator license is a supervisory credential and is not the same as a construction contractor registration.
Plumbers are regulated by the Alaska Department of Labor, Labor Standards and Safety, and those worker credentials are outside this CBPL source entry.
Construction contractor registration records are tracked separately from this mechanical administrator source entry.
The live search and verification endpoints may be protected by technical access controls that this project does not bypass.
rateLimitNotes: Use only official public lookup paths. Do not bypass technical controls or account-gated license-verification workflows.
publicRecordsNotes: Official lookup metadata only. Absence from this source is not proof that a mechanical administrator, plumbing worker credential, contractor registration, or local authorization does not exist elsewhere.
officialBulkDownloadNotes: No stable, lawful bulk export shape has been validated for this mechanical administrator source.
researchNotes: The official program page lists heating, cooling, process piping, HVAC, plumbing, sheet metal, and refrigeration categories. Alaska contractor regulations also define mechanical work by reference to the Uniform Swimming Pool, Spa, and Hot Tub Code and Uniform Solar Energy Code. Automated lookup/download support remains blocked pending lawful source-shape validation.
verificationCaveats: This source covers Alaska CBPL Mechanical Administrator records, not Department of Labor plumber credentials or local permits.
No matching record means no match in this source at the checked time, not proof that a mechanical or plumbing authorization does not exist elsewhere.
verificationNotes: No supported sync or verification adapter is exposed while official source access remains unresolved.
maintainerNotes: Keep Mechanical Administrator records separate from construction contractor registrations and Department of Labor worker credentials.

researchEvidence.note: Official CBPL program page reviewed; the page states the Mechanical Administrator program is staffed by CBPL and lists heating, cooling, process piping, residential HVAC, plumbing, HVAC/sheet metal, and refrigeration categories.
Official contractor statutes and regulations reviewed; the mechanical-contractor definition references the Uniform Swimming Pool, Spa, and Hot Tub Code and Uniform Solar Energy Code.

blocker.summary: Official lookup/download automation has not been validated against a stable lawful source shape, and protected workflows must not be bypassed.
